question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid consensusTimestamp values by probing findById() with sequential Long values via the opcode debug API, mapping the full set of gossiped contract transactions and identifying which transactions exist in the mirror node, causing complete transaction-graph disclosure that enables targeted gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated findById() calls with non-existent consensusTimestamp values at high frequency, causing sustained database query load that degrades the mirror node's ability to process and persist incoming gossiped contract transactions, effectively preventing new transactions from being recorded?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on findById() lookups to flood the database connection pool with concurrent timestamp queries, exhausting available connections and blocking the importer from writing newly gossiped ContractResult records, causing a denial of gossip persistence?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted consensusTimestamp of Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE to findById(), triggering unexpected SQL behavior or integer overflow in the underlying query that corrupts the database cursor state and prevents subsequent legitimate gossip-derived ContractResult lookups from succeeding?
